title: meeting-to-artifacts configuration
description: Schema for meeting-artifacts.config.yaml at the target repo's root. Every field is optional; the skill falls back to the documented defaults (see config.example.yaml, reference/routing.md, reference/providers.md) when the file or a field is absent.

filesystemDestination.description: A plain filesystem path, not necessarily version-controlled.

filesystemGitDestination.description: A path inside a git repo (this repo by default, or another one named via `repo`). The default interface for most artifact types.

repo.description: owner/name of the repo to write into. Omit to use the current repo.

style.description: plain: write pathPattern as-is. decision-log: this repo's ADR convention — one decision-<nnn>.md per decision under `dir`, plus a row appended to `dir`/decisions.md.

pathPattern.description: Required when style is plain. Supports {date}, {slug}, {seriesSlug}, {id} placeholders.

dir.description: Required when style is decision-log: directory holding decision-<nnn>.md and the decisions.md index.

mcpDestination.description: Interface via an already-configured MCP server. Recommended for any provider beyond plain filesystem (issue trackers, Notion, Google Docs, etc.) since it needs no bespoke integration code in the skill.

server.description: Name of the configured MCP server to write through, e.g. github, notion, google-docs.

resourceHint.description: Free-form hint for where within the server this goes (a repo, a Notion database/page id, a Docs folder).

labels.description: Applied when the server supports labeling (e.g. issue trackers).

cliDestination.description: Interface via a CLI already available in the environment. Use when no MCP server exists for the target system.

command.description: CLI invocation template used to write the artifact, e.g. `gh issue create ...`.

notes.description: Anything the harness needs to know about auth/flags for this CLI.

artifactDestination.description: One destination per artifact type. See reference/routing.md for which kind to pick per artifact.

providerInterface.description: How to talk to a configured provider (any input source or external system). MCP or CLI is recommended; api is only appropriate when the API is well-documented and public.

kind.description: mcp/cli are preferred — no bespoke integration to maintain. api requires apiDocsUrl and should only be used when no MCP/CLI integration exists.

server.description: Required when kind is mcp: the configured MCP server name.

command.description: Required when kind is cli: the CLI invocation template.

apiDocsUrl.description: Required when kind is api: link to the provider's public API documentation. Do not configure kind: api against an undocumented/private API.

provider.description: The input source the raw material (e.g. a transcript) is pulled from. A general pattern, not meeting-specific. Free-form rather than a fixed enum, on purpose: the harness (Claude/Cursor/etc.) reads the name and the paired `interface` block and figures out how to talk to it, using judgement rather than schema validation as the gate. See reference/providers.md.

name.description: Source name, e.g. zoom, google-meet, teams. Only zoom has actually been exercised so far — if this names a source with no working interface configured/available, the harness should say so and ask rather than guessing at an integration.

storage.description: Per-artifact-type destination. Unset types fall back to the filesystem-git defaults in reference/routing.md.

knowledgeBase.description: Optional pointer to the knowledge-management skill's vault, used to resolve mentions (services, people) during extraction and as the context.md routing target. See reference/knowledge-base-compat.md.

path.description: Vault root (the knowledge-management skill's vault.path; default knowledge/). A plain path or external reference also works when no vault exists.

notes.description: Free-form notes on how to query it.

series.description: Recurring meetings this skill tracks, for pre-reads and the cancellation-candidate audit.

slug.description: Stable identifier used in path patterns and to look up the previous occurrence.

publishChannel.description: Where to post pre-reads and post-meeting summaries for attendee visibility, e.g. a Slack channel. Free-form; requires the corresponding integration.

effectivenessLog.description: Path the per-meeting scorecard entries accumulate in.

scorecardCadence.description: How often to proactively suggest generating a rolled-up scorecard. on-request never triggers automatically.
